hero.headline: I build interfaces that work — and that stick.
hero.sub: Frontend Engineer — UI/UX · Cloud · Security · AI

about.label: Manifesto
about.headline: I'm obsessed with that last 10% that turns a functional UI into one people remember.
about.body: I don't deliver pages — I deliver products with weight, intention, and zero unnecessary friction. Every project is an artifact: built to last, built to convert, built to be used. Through Noctra Studio, I work with startups, independent professionals (doctors, lawyers, therapists), small businesses, schools, and real estate companies across LATAM that need serious digital presence without enterprise pricing.

projects.label: Projects shipped
projects.context: USAA, T-Mobile, Linio, Bridge Capital, and more

principles.title: Details aren't extra. They're the product.
principles.body: The last 10% is what separates a good UI from one that gets remembered. I don't stop at functional — I stop at right.

principles.title: Security by default, not by checklist.
principles.body: I build with OWASP, RLS, and rate-limiting from the start. A beautiful interface that leaks data is a liability, not a product.

principles.title: I own the problem, not just the task.
principles.body: I don't hand off tasks. I take problems, disappear for a while, and return with something better than what was asked for.

experience.label: Trajectory
experience.headline: The journey so far.
experience.intro: From building e-commerce at scale to leading UI systems at a global firm — and eventually founding my own studio. Each role taught me something the previous one couldn't.

0.description: Web development agency building performant digital products for startups, professionals, and businesses across LATAM.
0.highlight: I am currently working on 2 projects in the first 6 months. Simultaneously developing an in-house tooling ecosystem to eliminate friction between clients and the agency.
details: Noctra Discovery — A dynamic discovery form platform clients can fill from any device. Replaces the broken download-edit-save-reattach workflow — reducing duplication, delays, and errors at the most critical stage of a project.

Noctra Ops — An internal system for dynamically generating proposals, contracts, and cancellation agreements (before and during development). Legally grounded, designed to give clients security and confidence from the first document.

Noctra Forge (Native AI CRM) — A CRM built from scratch using real pain points from HubSpot and Pipedrive as reference. AI is native to the workflow, not bolted on. Adaptable to each client type, minimal learning curve. Estimated release: Q1 2027.

1.description: While my role evolved to support the Publishing team during a large-scale AEM migration, I took the initiative to act as the primary technical bridge between content and development teams.
1.highlight: Designed and implemented a comprehensive onboarding and mentorship program from scratch — technical documentation and guides that reduced ramp-up time and recurring errors.
details: Proactively investigated and pre-diagnosed performance issues and bugs within AEM's React-based components, collaborating with developers to accelerate the resolution cycle.
Conceptualized an internal knowledge base portal to centralize documentation and streamline processes.
Designed the security architecture proposal for that portal, including SSO and role-based access control.

2.description: Collaborated on migrating the T-Mobile web application from AngularJS to Angular 7, then expanded into ServiceNow development across DevOps and monitoring workflows.
2.highlight: Led production incident response — root-cause analysis, cross-team coordination, and high availability maintenance.
details: Refactored components and wrote unit tests with Jest/Mocha within a microservices architecture.
Customized Incident, CMDB and Service Catalog modules using Business Rules and Script Includes.
Expanded technical scope into Splunk/Grafana workflows to strengthen observability and incident triage.

3.description: Joined Quiubas Mobile before its Twilio acquisition. Contributed to the front-end team rewriting the entire client-side codebase from AngularJS to vanilla React.js.
3.highlight: Built functional components with the Hooks API and helped establish a cleaner, more testable frontend foundation — shipped with zero regressions during acquisition.
details: Implemented components with useState/useEffect to simplify side-effects and local state management.
Structured complex responsive layouts with CSS Grid without relying on third-party layout libraries.
Worked in an Agile cadence with daily stand-ups and two-week sprints to ship incremental improvements.

4.description: Where I discovered software engineering. Moved beyond content management into internal tooling and process improvements for a platform serving millions of users.
4.highlight: Built an internal email-generation tool using Twig (PHP) while managing multi-regional web content.
details: Managed website content operations using HTML, CSS and JavaScript across multiple regions.
Gained first hands-on experience with AWS and web operations, reinforcing my transition into full-time engineering.

agency.label: Agency
agency.headline: Need to build something?
agency.description_line_1: We work with startups, firms, clinics, schools, and businesses across LATAM.

bridgeCapital.overview: Bridge Capital needed a bilingual investment portfolio shipped in under three weeks, with premium visual credibility and mobile-first performance for high-net-worth visitors.

painPoints.title: No reusable component foundation
painPoints.description: The initial Figma handoff had inconsistent spacing, duplicate button styles, and no typography scale. Every section behaved like a one-off implementation.
painPoints.fix: Established design tokens and atomic primitives first, then assembled page sections from reusable components. This reduced implementation friction and review cycles.

painPoints.title: No internationalization architecture
painPoints.description: The project started as Spanish-only. Midway through delivery, the client requested a full English version without delaying launch.
painPoints.fix: Integrated next-intl with locale-aware routing and dictionary-based content. This allowed language expansion without template rewrites.

painPoints.title: Unoptimized assets hurting LCP
painPoints.description: Initial visual assets were heavy and loaded without prioritization, increasing above-the-fold render time on mobile networks.
painPoints.fix: Compressed assets, used Next.js Image priorities, and staged loading for non-critical visuals to improve perceived and measured speed.

testimonial.quote: We expected a landing page. What we got was a scalable product foundation we could keep building on.
testimonial.author: Bridge Capital Team

woodaxDesign.overview: Woodax Design needed a product-first brand experience that felt premium while maintaining strict technical quality on performance and layout stability.

painPoints.title: Dense layout reduced product clarity
painPoints.description: Early drafts mixed brand narrative and catalog content in the same viewport, forcing users to work too hard to find products.
painPoints.fix: Rebuilt page rhythm with modular content blocks, stronger spacing hierarchy, and clear product CTA anchors.

painPoints.title: Layout shifts during image load
painPoints.description: Hero and gallery content moved while assets loaded, creating a visibly unstable experience on slower devices.
painPoints.fix: Set stable media containers with explicit dimensions and deferred non-critical media. This removed visual jumping.

painPoints.title: Typography lost legibility on small screens
painPoints.description: Display typography looked strong on desktop but compressed too tightly on mobile, harming readability and trust.
painPoints.fix: Introduced fluid type scaling and tightened the line-length system for mobile-first readability.

noctraStudio.overview: Noctra Studio's own site had to function as a conversion asset, a trust layer, and a demonstration of technical depth — for startup founders, professionals, and business owners alike.

painPoints.title: Low-trust first impression
painPoints.description: The previous version looked generic and lacked clear proof points, making agency differentiation difficult in the first 10 seconds.
painPoints.fix: Reframed the homepage around outcomes, process clarity, and strategic visual hierarchy to build instant credibility.

painPoints.title: Mobile entry speed below expectations
painPoints.description: Mobile audiences encountered slow hero rendering due to unoptimized visual assets and excessive animation on load.
painPoints.fix: Prioritized above-fold rendering, reduced expensive effects, and optimized media strategy for critical paths.

painPoints.title: Service offer felt ambiguous
painPoints.description: Visitors could not quickly understand whether Noctra handled strategy, engineering, or only design deliverables.
painPoints.fix: Structured the narrative around clear scope blocks, delivery model, and engagement CTAs tied to business outcomes.

testimonial.quote: Noctra's own site became our best closer. Prospects arrived already aligned with how we build.
testimonial.author: Manu de Quevedo

thisPortfolio.overview: This portfolio was designed as a proof-of-work system: every section demonstrates frontend craft, performance discipline, and product storytelling in a single experience.

painPoints.title: Template-like portfolio structure
painPoints.description: Most portfolio layouts looked interchangeable and failed to communicate technical depth in the first interaction.
painPoints.fix: Built section-specific interaction patterns, narrative pacing, and distinctive typography to make each scroll segment intentional.

painPoints.title: Manual CV delivery flow
painPoints.description: Recruiters needed bilingual CV outputs while prior workflows required manual file updates and inconsistent exports.
painPoints.fix: Implemented dynamic PDF generation endpoint with locale-aware output and direct download actions from navigation.

painPoints.title: Desktop-first interactions leaked into mobile
painPoints.description: Hover-driven details were inconsistent on touch devices and degraded perceived polish on mobile.
painPoints.fix: Added capability-based interaction guards and touch-first variants for decorative effects and motion cues.

skills.label: Arsenal
skills.headline: The tools I master.
skills.footnote: (Open to learning new technologies and adapting quickly to change.)

contact.label: Contact
contact.headline: If you're building something — or just know you need something better — I want to hear about it.
contact.subtext: Currently open to full-time roles and selective collaborations. Based in Querétaro, MX — working remotely worldwide.
